docs.title: Hexer
docs.text: A light-weight and easy to deploy application which leverages Golang to expose a TUI for organization of HTB sessions.

docs.title: About
docs.text: Tired of having notes for HTB scattered all about? Want a quick way to get your /etc/hosts file setup? Use hexer.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text:
Get up and running quick like:

go run github.com/archimoebius/hexer@latest serve

docs.title: Install / Setup
docs.text:
To download hexer and then use it - something like the following will do:

go install github.com/archimoebius/hexer@latest
hexer serve

docs.title: Quick Start - Release Binary
docs.text:
1. Download a release

  2. copy it to your desired location

  3. Jump to 'Configuration'

docs.title: Quick Start - I'm'a Developer!
docs.text:
1. git clone git@github.com:ArchiMoebius/hexer.git && cd hexer

  2. make

  3. copy ./dist/temp/flavor to your desired location

  4. Jump to 'Configuration'

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
You can use a combination of CLI Parameters, environment variables, .hexer.yaml, and default settings to get up and running. The precedence for application of state is as follows:

    1: CLI Parameters
    2: environment
    3: .hexer.yaml
    4: defaults

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: The simplest way to deploy Hexer is by using the 'local' mode - no SSH server is setup, and upon execution you are dropped into a TUI.

docs.title: Setup with SSH
docs.text:
When using the SSH 'server' mode - there are two magic users.

register and administrate - more on those in a second.

First see the following example of a .hexer.yaml configuration file.

storage: ./data
ssh-user: user
ssh-key: >
  -----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
  b3BlbnNzaC1rZXktdjEAAAAABG5vbmUAAAAEbm9uZQAAAAAAAAABAAAAMwAAAAtzc2gtZW

port: 2222

theme: # base from https://ethanschoonover.com/solarized/
  style:
    header_text:

        dark: "#859900"

          dark: "#2380C2"
      normal_description:
        foreground:

          dark: "#457A9F"

          dark: "#585FD8"
        border_foreground:
          light: "#5960D7"
          dark: "#5960D7"
      selected_description:
        foreground:
          light: "#6C71C4"
          dark: "#6C71C4"
      dimmed_title:
        foreground:
          light: "#AB4BB0"
          dark: "#AB4BB0"
      dimmed_description:

          light: "#8B3D8F"
          dark: "#8B3D8F"

Run the server - and then we'll register our first users.

hexer serve

docs.title: Register the first user
docs.text:
Registration always uses the username of register - and the SSH keypair you wish to register a username for.

The first user to register the administrate handle - gains the priviledge of administration over all users which are registered. It's recommended to register this user as soon as the server is setup. This is accomplished as follows:

1) Create a SSH keypair for the administrate user

```bash
ssh-keygen -f $HOME/.ssh/id_hexer_administrate
```

2) Fill in the form with the username/handle of administrate to setup the special user - this one can manage the registration status (and deletion) of accounts.

```bash
ssh -i $HOME/.ssh/id_hexer_administrate -p 2222 register@127.0.0.1
```

3) Create a SSH keypair for the your first user

```bash
ssh-keygen -f $HOME/.ssh/id_hexer_username_here
```

4) Register the first user

```bash
ssh -i $HOME/.ssh/id_hexer_username_here -p 2222 register@127.0.0.1
```

5) Using the administrate account - verify or delete the first user - note: we're using the administrate account for the first time!

```bash
ssh -i $HOME/.ssh/id_hexer_administrate -p 2222 administrate@127.0.0.1
```

6) Login as the first user

```bash
ssh -i $HOME/.ssh/id_hexer_username_here -p 2222 username_here@127.0.0.1
```

docs.title: hexer
docs.text: A place to organize HTB activities

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Leverage SSH or just stay local - an organize those HTB labs

hexer [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
-c, --config string         .hexer.yaml (default ".hexer.yaml")
  -d, --debug                 Output debug information
  -h, --help                  help for hexer
  -l, --log-basepath string   The base filepath where logs will be stored (default "./")
  -v, --version               Show the version and exit

docs.title: SEE ALSO
docs.text:
• hexer completion - Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
  • hexer doc - Build documentation under docs/
  • hexer execute - Wrap and collect executing command and resulting output
  • hexer note - Record history as notes for a project
  • hexer serve - Start the server on the desired ip/port

  • hexer completion bash - Generate the autocompletion script for bash

docs.title: hexer completion bash
docs.text: Generate the autocompletion script for bash

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Generate the autocompletion script for the bash shell.

This script depends on the 'bash-completion' package. If it is not installed already, you can install it via your OS's package manager.

To load completions in your current shell session:

source <(hexer completion bash)

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

docs.title: Linux:
docs.text: hexer completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/hexer

docs.title: macOS:
docs.text:
hexer completion bash > $(brew --prefix)/etc/bash_completion.d/hexer

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

hexer completion bash

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
-h, --help              help for bash
      --no-descriptions   disable completion descriptions

docs.title: Options inherited from parent commands
docs.text:
-c, --config string         .hexer.yaml (default ".hexer.yaml")
  -d, --debug                 Output debug information
  -l, --log-basepath string   The base filepath where logs will be stored (default "./")

docs.title: SEE ALSO
docs.text: • hexer completion - Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell

docs.title: hexer completion fish
docs.text: Generate the autocompletion script for fish

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Generate the autocompletion script for the fish shell.

To load completions in your current shell session:

hexer completion fish | source

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

hexer completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/hexer.fish

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

hexer completion fish [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
-h, --help              help for fish
      --no-descriptions   disable completion descriptions

docs.title: hexer completion powershell
docs.text: Generate the autocompletion script for powershell

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Generate the autocompletion script for powershell.

To load completions in your current shell session:

hexer completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

To load completions for every new session, add the output of the above command to your powershell profile.

hexer completion powershell [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
-h, --help              help for powershell
      --no-descriptions   disable completion descriptions

docs.title: hexer completion zsh
docs.text: Generate the autocompletion script for zsh

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Generate the autocompletion script for the zsh shell.

If shell completion is not already enabled in your environment you will need to enable it. You can execute the following once:

echo "autoload -U compinit; compinit" >> ~/.zshrc

To load completions in your current shell session:

source <(hexer completion zsh)

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

docs.title: Linux:
docs.text: hexer completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_hexer"

docs.title: macOS:
docs.text:
hexer completion zsh > $(brew --prefix)/share/zsh/site-functions/_hexer

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

hexer completion zsh [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
-h, --help              help for zsh
      --no-descriptions   disable completion descriptions

docs.title: hexer note
docs.text: Record history as notes for a project

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Having used the 'execute' command to record execution, record the results to a project

hexer note [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--export                Instead of uploading notes - download them
  -h, --help                  help for note
      --ip string             The IP to listen on for SSH connections - if not set, will bind to 127.0.0.1 (if not local) (default "127.0.0.1")
      --local                 Don't start an SSH handler for the TUI, just run the TUI locally
      --port string           The port to listen on for SSH connections - if not set, will bind to a random high port (if not local) (default "2222")
      --project-id string     The project ID to record notes under
      --ssh-key string        The private SSH key to leverage to connecting (if not local)
      --ssh-user string       The private SSH user to leverage to connecting (if not local)
      --storage_path string   The folder to store the database in - (if local) (default "./storage")

docs.title: Options inherited from parent commands
docs.text:
-c, --config string         .hexer.yaml (default ".hexer.yaml")
  -d, --debug                 Output debug information

docs.title: hexer serve
docs.text: Start the server on the desired ip/port

docs.title: Synopsis
docs.text:
Leveraging the goodness of Golang and SSH - setup a local server for recording HTB credentials & flags

hexer serve [flags]

docs.title: Options
docs.text:
--database_ip string     The IP to listen on for Database connections (default "127.0.0.1")
      --database_port string   The port to listen on for Database connections (default "8090")
  -h, --help                   help for serve
      --ip string              The IP to listen on for SSH connections (default "127.0.0.1")
      --local                  Don't start an SSH handler for the TUI, just run the TUI locally
      --open_registration      Allow any user to register - auto approve the account
      --port string            The port to listen on for SSH connections (default "2222")
      --storage_path string    The folder to store the database in (default "./storage")

docs.title: Options inherited from parent commands
docs.text:
-c, --config string         .hexer.yaml (default ".hexer.yaml")
